message:
fix(extensions_js,installer): preserve extension ID in timer callbacks, installer TTY guard

Timer extension ID context:
- extensions_js.rs: setTimeout and setInterval callbacks now capture
  __pi_current_extension_id at registration time and restore it during
  callback execution via try/finally — prevents hostcalls from timer
  callbacks being attributed to the wrong extension

Installer fixes:
- install.sh: add [ -t 1 ] TTY check to run_with_spinner() gum path
  so spinner is suppressed when stdout is piped/redirected
- Split local declaration from assignment in verify_sigstore_bundle()
  for shellcheck SC2155 compliance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -134,7 +134,7 @@ err() {
 run_with_spinner() {
   local title="$1"
   shift
-  if [ "$HAS_GUM" -eq 1 ] && [ "$NO_GUM" -eq 0 ] && [ "$QUIET" -eq 0 ]; then
+  if [ "$HAS_GUM" -eq 1 ] && [ "$NO_GUM" -eq 0 ] && [ "$QUIET" -eq 0 ] && [ -t 1 ]; then
     gum spin --spinner dot --title "$title" -- "$@"
   else
     # Keep status text off stdout so callers can safely capture command output.
@@ -917,7 +917,8 @@ verify_sigstore_bundle() {
     bundle_url="${artifact_base}.sigstore.json"
   fi
 
-  local bundle_file="$TMP/$(basename "${bundle_url%%\?*}")"
+  local bundle_file
+  bundle_file="$TMP/$(basename "${bundle_url%%\?*}")"
   if ! curl -fsSL "$bundle_url" -o "$bundle_file"; then
     SIGSTORE_STATUS="skipped (bundle unavailable)"
     warn "Sigstore bundle not found; skipping signature verification"
